propositions: Agent guidance is Claude-locked and bloated, with CLAUDE.md duplicating large parts of docs/ and degrading instruction-following.
AGENTS.md should become the canonical root guidance file, with a thin CLAUDE.md bridging Claude Code via a one-line @AGENTS.md import.
Portable rebar-usage guidance should be pushed into docs/ as the single source of truth, with thin audience-specific files pointing at it.
A provider-neutral templates/AGENTS.md must let client projects teach their agents to drive rebar, kept in sync by a delimited-region drift-guard test.
Example skills must be de-branded and generalized into a provider-neutral directory renamed from examples/claude-skills while keeping SKILL.md format.

The provider-config scaffolding (.codex/config.toml, .agents/rules/rebar.md) is untracked and locally excluded, so migration is effectively greenfield and these files must be brought under version control.
